questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Cell is the structural and functional unit of life.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has all the organelles necessary for growth, reproduction, and maintenance of life.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. It contains all the necessary organelles for growth, reproduction, and maintenance of life, making this statement true.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): All cells have a true nucleus.
questions.reason: Reason (R): All cells have a well-defined nuclear envelope and chromatin.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: All eukaryotic cells have a true nucleus with a well-defined nuclear envelope and chromatin, making this statement true.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Eukaryotic cells have a large cytoplasm.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Eukaryotic cells have a true nucleus and other organelles, which occupy a significant portion of the cell.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Eukaryotic cells have a true nucleus and other organelles, which occupy a significant portion of the cell. This makes the cytoplasm relatively large compared to prokaryotic cells.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Mitochondria are present in all cells.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Mitochondria are the site of cellular respiration.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are not present in all cells, specifically in red blood cells of mammals and some other cells. They are indeed the site of cellular respiration.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The genetic information of a cell is contained in the mitochondria.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Mitochondria have their own DNA, mtDNA, which is separate from the nuclear DNA.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The genetic information of a cell is primarily contained in the nucleus, not in the mitochondria. Although mitochondria have their own DNA, mtDNA, it is not the primary site of genetic information.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The cell wall is a rigid structure found in plant cells.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It provides support and protection to the cell.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The cell wall is a rigid structure found in plant cells, and it provides support and protection to the cell, making this statement true.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Chloroplasts are found in all plant cells.
questions.reason: Reason (R): They are the site of photosynthesis in plants.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts are not found in all plant cells; they are absent in root cells and some other cells. They are indeed the site of photosynthesis in plants.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Centrioles are found in all animal cells.
questions.reason: Reason (R): They play a crucial role in the formation of cilia and flagella.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Centrioles are found in many animal cells, but not all. They play a crucial role in the formation of cilia and flagella, making this statement partially true.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Lysosomes are found in all eukaryotic cells.
questions.reason: Reason (R): They contain digestive enzymes that break down waste materials.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are not found in all eukaryotic cells, specifically in red blood cells of mammals and some other cells. They indeed contain digestive enzymes that break down waste materials.
